description:
## Problem

An agent reading the `bl skill` output naturally lands on the Commands table:

> bl review TASK_ID -m "msg" — Squash to main, set status=review. Worktree stays.

Nothing in that row says the task id is auto-appended to the commit subject. The auto-append fact lives further down under "Commit messages: 50/72 shape" ("The delivery tag `[bl-xxxx]` is appended to the title automatically"), which a skimming agent will miss.

Meanwhile every squash on main shows `[bl-xxxx]` in the subject, so the natural inference is "that's the convention, mirror it in -m". An agent that does this gets duplicated tags in the squash subject (e.g. `…title [bl-fdb9] [bl-fdb9]`).

## Reproduction

Real incident: agent ran `bl review bl-fdb9 -m "...§11/§12 [bl-fdb9]"`. Resulting main squash subject carried two `[bl-fdb9]` suffixes.

## Fix (preferred: doc-only)

Add the auto-append warning to the `bl review` row of the Commands table in the skill template, e.g.:

> bl review TASK_ID -m "msg" — Squash to main, set status=review. Worktree stays. The task id is auto-appended to the subject; do NOT include [bl-xxxx] in your message.

Rationale for doc-only over a DWIM behavior change: the auto-append guarantee is good (every squash carries the id even if the human forgets); fixing the doc is cheaper and the gap is one row, not the underlying behavior.

## Out of scope

Behavior change (detect-and-skip on duplicate trailing tag) is the alternative. Possible follow-up if the doc fix proves insufficient, but not part of this task.

## Acceptance

- The `bl review` row in the skill Commands table mentions the auto-append and warns against including [bl-xxxx] in -m.
- `bl skill` output reflects it.
- A test exists that asserts the warning is present in the rendered skill output (so this can't regress silently).
title: Skill doc: warn that bl review auto-appends [bl-xxxx]
